title: Today's Tech Brief: Microsoft's 0-Day Feud and Apple's AI Plans
summary: A disgruntled 0-day hunter's public feud with Microsoft, Apple's plans to integrate Google's Gemini AI into iPhones, and more.

body.text: I'm Leo Stone, and welcome to today's tech brief. We're looking at some interesting developments in the world of technology. I've got a couple of stories that caught my eye recently, and I think you'll find them pretty fascinating. First up, a public feud between a disgruntled 0-day hunter and Microsoft. Then, Apple's plans to supercharge Siri with Google's Gemini AI. Let's dive in.

The 0-day hunter, who wishes to remain anonymous, claims to have found several zero-day vulnerabilities in Windows and is threatening to release more on July 14th. According to a report from The Register, the researcher says they were 'humiliated' by Microsoft and is planning a 'bone-shattering drop' of exploits. Microsoft, on the other hand, has called in law enforcement. This cat-and-mouse game between security researchers and tech giants can get pretty intense. It's a delicate balance between finding vulnerabilities and patching them.

I've been following this story, and it's clear that the researcher is upset about how Microsoft handled their findings. On the other hand, Microsoft has to prioritize keeping their users safe. It's a tough situation for both parties. I'm curious to see how it all plays out.

Disgruntled 0-day hunter 'humiliated' by Microsoft pledges 'bone shattering drop' as Redmond calls cops is also worth a quick mention. The main content says: Security Disgruntled 0-day hunter 'humiliated' by Microsoft pledges 'bone shattering drop' as Redmond calls cops Six 0-days, three under active exploitation, more to come on July 14? The point is not to force it into a giant theory; it is simply part of today's news mix.

Apple working to cram massive Gemini model into iPhone to power new Siri is also worth a quick mention. The main content says: Text settings Story text Size Small Standard Large Width * Standard Wide Links Standard Orange * Subscribers only Learn more Minimize to nav It’s impossible to totally avoid generative AI when interacting with technology anymore, but Apple has a bit less of it. That’s not entirely by choice, though. The iPhone maker has delayed the AI-enhanced Siri multiple... The point is not to force it into a giant theory; it is simply part of today's news mix.

My quick read is that today's set of stories is useful because it gives us texture, not a sermon. Each item has its own little job: one tells us what changed, another tells us what people are worried about, and another gives the day its mood.

Apple working to cram massive Gemini model into iPhone to power new Siri
